source_jobs.title: Computational Biologist
source_jobs.description:
Bio is a decentralized science protocol that helps launch and grow AI-driven biotech research. It enables scientists to raise funds, create value from their work, and distribute that value directly to their communities. Since 2023, Bio has directed over $50m to global researchers, offering an alternative to traditional pharma funding. Backed by investors like Binance Labs, Northpond Ventures, and Animoca Brands, Bio accelerates real-world therapeutics across longevity, brain health, fertility, psychedelic science, and more.




THE ROLE

We're looking for a computational biologist to serve as the bridge between our AI systems and physical reality. We need someone who can programmatically QA the high-volume outputs of our AI before they reach the bench - catching errors that are invisible to humans but obvious in the data.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

• Validate AI outputs using computational methods - simulations, structural analysis, database cross-referencing

• Translate AI predictions into executable protocols for cloud labs (Emerald, Strateos) or technical specs for CROs

• Build and maintain validation pipelines that catch errors before they waste lab resources

• Manage the "hand-off" between digital predictions and physical experiments

• Transform JSON outputs from our agents into experimental protocols and validation workflows




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

• PhD in Computational Biology, Bioinformatics, or related field preferred - or equivalent exceptional experience

• Strong proficiency in Python and/or R for data analysis and pipeline development

• Experience with computational validation methods (molecular simulations, structural analysis, sequence analysis)

• Ability to critically evaluate biological predictions and identify potential issues

• Comfort working at the interface of software and biology




NICE TO HAVE

• Experience with cloud lab platforms (Emerald, Strateos, or similar)

• Background working with CROs or translating computational work to wet-lab execution

• Familiarity with AI/ML systems and their failure modes

• Experience red-teaming or adversarially testing scientific predictions




WHY BIO

• Work at the cutting edge of AI-driven biology

• Fully remote - work from anywhere

• Small team where your expertise directly shapes our science

• Competitive compensation with token/equity component

source_jobs.title: Product Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Bio is a decentralized science protocol that helps launch and grow AI-driven biotech research. It enables scientists to raise funds, create value from their work, and distribute that value directly to their communities. Since 2023, Bio has directed over $50m to global researchers, offering an alternative to traditional pharma funding. Backed by investors like Binance Labs, Northpond Ventures, and Animoca Brands, Bio accelerates real-world therapeutics across longevity, brain health, fertility, psychedelic science, and more.




THE ROLE

Our core team is focused on improving our AI agents' reasoning - we need someone dedicated to rapidly building the apps and wrappers that put those agents in users' hands. You'll take custom use cases (personal health data analysis, bio-database exploration, and more) from idea to deployed MVP in days, not months.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

• Ship deployed, user-facing MVPs fast - comfortable with "disposable code" to test market value

• Build connectors and integrations for messy external data sources (Fitbit, Whoop, EHRs, public bio-databases)

• Translate complex, non-deterministic AI outputs into clean, actionable UI/UX

• Prototype new use cases and validate them with real users

• Free up the core team to focus on the "Brain" while you own the "App"




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

• Track record of shipping products quickly - you've built and deployed things people actually use

• Full-stack skills with a bias toward frontend and user experience

• Fearless when it comes to integrating with messy, undocumented, or inconsistent APIs

• Strong product intuition - you care about what users need, not just what's technically interesting

• Comfortable with ambiguity and rapid iteration




NICE TO HAVE

• Experience building UIs for AI/ML systems or non-deterministic outputs

• Familiarity with health/fitness data platforms or healthcare APIs

• Background in design or strong eye for UI/UX

• Interest in biology, health tech, or personal analytics




WHY BIO

• High autonomy - own entire products from concept to launch

• Fully remote - work from anywhere

• Work at the intersection of AI and biology

• Competitive compensation with token/equity component

source_jobs.title: AI Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Bio is a decentralized science protocol that helps launch and grow AI-driven biotech research. It enables scientists to raise funds, create value from their work, and distribute that value directly to their communities. Since 2023, Bio has directed over $50m to global researchers, offering an alternative to traditional pharma funding. Backed by investors like Binance Labs, Northpond Ventures, and Animoca Brands, Bio accelerates real-world therapeutics across longevity, brain health, fertility, psychedelic science, and more.




WHY THIS ROLE EXISTS

As a Member of Technical Staff on the AI Agents team, you’ll design, build, and scale the core agent systems that power Bio Protocol’s products. You’ll work closely with full-stack engineers and scientist-evaluators to create agents that can plan, use tools, and reason safely. This role offers the opportunity to shape the foundation of how AI collaborates with human scientists - combining technical depth with real-world scientific impact. While technical skills matter, we believe drive and cultural fit matter most. If you're passionate about shipping impactful work and excited by our mission, we encourage you to apply - even if you don't check every single box.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Build agent capabilities for planning, tool use, memory, and context management, and ship them into production.

 - Integrate agents with internal and external tools and data sources (retrieval systems, structured datasets, lab/biomed APIs, spreadsheets, search), with robust schemas and safeguards.

 - Develop quality and evaluation systems, including unit, regression, and scenario/benchmark tests, telemetry, and automated scoring.

 - Collaborate with scientists to analyze failure modes and improve performance.

 - Partner with the knowledge and ontology team to ensure outputs are source-traceable and compliant with provenance standards.

 - Implement safety measures, guardrails, and sandboxed execution for risky operations.

 - Optimize performance and reliability through profiling, idempotency, retries, rate limiting, and uptime management.

 - Instrument data pipelines for supervised fine-tuning and reinforcement learning when needed.

 - Contribute to the agent platform, including services, APIs, orchestration, CI/CD, and observability.




EXAMPLE PROJECTS (FIRST 90 DAYS)

 - Deliver a multi-tool agent capable of executing long-horizon scientific tasks with memory and self-correction, supported by regression tests and telemetry.

 - Implement automated citation enforcement, including source checking, freshness validation, and provenance display in the UI.

 - Build an evaluation dashboard tracking competency pass rates, latency, and failure modes.

 - Success metrics

 - Improved pass rates and reduced critical error rates across core scientific competencies.

 - Performance against SLOs for latency, task success, tool-call reliability, and uptime.

 - Increased coverage of regression and evaluation scenarios.

 - Broader adoption of the agent platform by internal teams.




QUALIFICATIONS

 - Experience building production software in Python and/or TypeScript, with strong systems and API design skills (FastAPI, gRPC, GraphQL, or similar).

 - Proven experience shipping LLM applications or agentic systems (tool use/function calling, retrieval/RAG, structured outputs, evaluation, or observability).

 - Familiarity with agent/orchestration frameworks (e.g., LangChain, LangGraph, AutoGen, CrewAI, MCP) and vector databases (FAISS, Weaviate, Pinecone).

 - Experience with cloud infrastructure and containers (AWS, GCP, or Azure), Docker/Kubernetes/Terraform, CI/CD, and production telemetry.

 - Ability to translate research prototypes into robust, scalable systems.




NICE TO HAVE

 - Experience with fine-tuning and reinforcement learning (RL, RLAIF, RLHF), including reward design and offline evaluation.

 - Familiarity with benchmarks and evaluations such as SWE-Bench, OS-World, or tau-bench.

 - Kno

source_jobs.title: Open Application
source_jobs.description:
Bio is a decentralized science protocol that helps launch and grow AI-driven biotech research. It enables scientists to raise funds, create value from their work, and distribute that value directly to their communities. Since 2023, Bio has directed over $50m to global researchers, offering an alternative to traditional pharma funding. Backed by investors like Binance Labs, Northpond Ventures, and Animoca Brands, Bio accelerates real-world therapeutics across longevity, brain health, fertility, psychedelic science, and more.



Bio is building the infrastructure for decentralized science - from AI agents that accelerate research to the coordination tools that fund and validate it. We sit at the intersection of web3 and biotechnology, creating systems that let researchers, funders, and the scientific community collaborate without gatekeepers. Our stack spans smart contracts, agentic AI, cloud lab integrations, and the interfaces that tie them together.



If you've built complex technical systems, contributed meaningfully to open-source projects, shipped research into production, or mastered multiple domains, we want to hear from you. Whether your expertise is in computational biology, distributed systems, smart contracts, AI/ML, or something unexpected, what matters is your ability to learn fast, think rigorously, and execute.



We're a team with big ambitions. The people here have backgrounds spanning biotech, crypto, and frontier AI - united by the belief that science should be open, funding should be decentralized, and AI can accelerate discovery by orders of magnitude.



Tell us what excites you about Bio, something impressive you've built, and how you'd help accelerate open science.

source_jobs.title: Core Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Bio is a decentralized science protocol that helps launch and grow AI-driven biotech research. It enables scientists to raise funds, create value from their work, and distribute that value directly to their communities. Since 2023, Bio has directed over $50m to global researchers, offering an alternative to traditional pharma funding. Backed by investors like Binance Labs, Northpond Ventures, and Animoca Brands, Bio accelerates real-world therapeutics across longevity, brain health, fertility, psychedelic science, and more.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We're building next-generation infrastructure for decentralized science and are looking for a Core Engineer who has successfully built AI agent systems or complex research infrastructure from scratch. This is a foundational role where you'll own the design, architecture, and implementation of the core systems that power our AI agents and coordination protocols.

You'll work closely with product, research, and science teams to shape the technical direction of Bio and ensure our systems are reliable, performant, and ready to scale globally.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Architect and build high-performance AI agent infrastructure from the ground up

 - Design and implement on-chain coordination systems (funding mechanisms, governance, research incentives, token flows)

 - Build fault-tolerant backend services for agent orchestration, data pipelines, and integration with external systems (cloud labs, bio-databases, CROs)

 - Optimise system performance, throughput, and reliability under real-world research workloads

 - Lead technical decisions around system design, data models, and scaling strategy

 - Collaborate closely with product and science teams to translate research requirements into production systems

 - Establish best practices for code quality, testing, observability, and incident response

 - Mentor other engineers as the team grows




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - Proven experience building AI agent systems, research platforms, or complex technical infrastructure from scratch

 - Strong background in distributed systems and high-throughput architectures

 - Expertise in one or more backend languages (Python, Go, Rust, TypeScript)

 - Deep understanding of LLM architectures, agent orchestration patterns, and ML systems in production

 - Experience designing systems that integrate with messy, real-world data sources

 - Solid grasp of smart contract development and on-chain coordination mechanisms

 - Comfortable operating in an early-stage, fast-moving environment

 - Strong communication skills and ability to work cross-functionally




NICE TO HAVE

 - Experience scaling AI systems to production with thousands of users

 - Prior work in biotech, computational biology, or scientific computing

 - Background in DeSci, crypto protocols, or decentralized coordination

 - Open-source contributions in AI, research tooling, or infrastructure




WHY JOIN US

 - Foundational role with real technical ownership and impact

 - Competitive salary + meaningful equity/token component

 - Remote-first culture with a world-class, mission-driven team

 - Opportunity to build category-defining infrastructure for open science from day one

source_jobs.title: Chief Science Officer
source_jobs.description:
Bio is a decentralized science protocol that helps launch and grow AI-driven biotech research. It enables scientists to raise funds, create value from their work, and distribute that value directly to their communities. Since 2023, Bio has directed over $50m to global researchers, offering an alternative to traditional pharma funding. Backed by investors like Binance Labs, Northpond Ventures, and Animoca Brands, Bio accelerates real-world therapeutics across longevity, brain health, fertility, psychedelic science, and more.




WHY THIS ROLE EXISTS

Bio is entering a phase where scientific ambition must deliver repeatable, credible outcomes at portfolio scale. We need senior leadership to evaluate, prioritize, and support multiple assets while improving the systems that guide scientific decisions.

The CSO will own Bio's scientific direction at the portfolio level - ensuring that promising ideas are rigorously evaluated, translated into testable programs, and moved into labs. The right person brings deep scientific credibility, experience with drug development, and the ability to attract and guide top-tier researchers.

A key requirement is meaningful exposure to the drug development and approval process - having observed programs through preclinical, IND-enabling, clinical, and regulatory stages.




WHAT THE CSO WILL ACTUALLY DO


1. OWN BIO’S PORTFOLIO-LEVEL SCIENTIFIC DIRECTION

Review projects launching on Bio Protocol, maintain a clear portfolio view of what's strongest and most promising, and make judgment calls on what to prioritize and what not to pursue.




2. TRANSLATE AI OUTPUTS INTO LAB-VALIDATED SCIENCE

Work closely with Bio's AI team to take AI-generated hypotheses and move them into experimental validation. Design and oversee the processes that turn computational insights into real, testable scientific results - quickly and rigorously.




3. LEAD SCIENTIFIC DILIGENCE AND EVALUATION

Build scalable processes to assess scientific credibility, risk, and potential across projects. Translate reviews into clear recommendations for go/no-go decisions and portfolio prioritization.




4. RECRUIT AND SUPPORT TOP-TIER SCIENTISTS

Develop and execute a strategy to bring world-class researchers into Bio's ecosystem - leveraging academic networks, conferences, and partnerships. Serve as a scientific sparring partner to researchers, helping refine hypotheses and stress-test approaches.




5. REPRESENT BIO'S SCIENTIFIC CREDIBILITY EXTERNALLY

Act as Bio's scientific voice with academics, biotech operators, investors, and ecosystem partners. Stay current on scientific and biotech trends, and represent Bio at conferences and industry events.




6. BUILD AN AI-NATIVE SCIENTIFIC OPERATING MODEL

Work with Bio's systems and knowledge infrastructure to develop AI-driven workflows for research review, synthesis, and evaluation - so scientific judgment scales with the platform.




7. LEAD AND GROW THE SCIENCE TEAM

Hire, mentor, and manage Bio's Science team. Ensure the team operates at a high level and delivers on Bio's scientific mission.




THE PROFILE WE’RE LOOKING FOR

A strong scientific foundation (PhD or equivalent depth) combined with experience outside of pure academia. This person should have meaningful exposure to the drug development and approval process, having witnessed one or more programs progress through preclinical development, IND-enabling studies, clinical trials, and regulatory interaction even if they were not directly executing the program.




EXPERIENCE THAT REALLY MATTERS

 - Senior scientific leadership, ideally with portfolio-level exposure (e.g., biotech fund, biotech company with multiple programs, or similar environment)

 - Demonstrated understanding of drug development realities, regulatory risk, and translational challenges

 - Track record of attracting, advising, or leading researchers and scientific teams

 - Comfort operating at a strategic level while staying close to the science

 - Int

source_jobs.title: E-commerce Lead
source_jobs.description:
Bio is a decentralized science protocol that helps launch and grow AI-driven biotech research. It enables scientists to raise funds, create value from their work, and distribute that value directly to their communities. Since 2023, Bio has directed over $50m to global researchers, offering an alternative to traditional pharma funding. Backed by investors like Binance Labs, Northpond Ventures, and Animoca Brands, Bio accelerates real-world therapeutics across longevity, brain health, fertility, psychedelic science, and more.







ABOUT BIOFY

Biofy is Bio Protocol's commerce layer - a marketplace where cutting-edge, research-backed products created by decentralized science communities (BioDAOs) meet consumers. From longevity supplements to brain-health formulations, Biofy bridges the gap between decentralized research and real-world impact. We're building a new kind of e-commerce platform where product provenance is tracked on-chain and every purchase supports scientific innovation.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We're looking for an E-Commerce Lead to own customer acquisition and drive growth for Biofy Shop. You'll operate across paid and organic channels - social, search, email, website - with an obsessive focus on optimization, reach, and conversion. This is a hands-on role for someone with D2C experience who knows how to acquire customers profitably and iterate daily as channels evolve. While experience matters, we believe drive and cultural fit matter most - if you're excited about our mission and passionate about shipping, we encourage you to apply even if you don't meet every requirement.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Own customer acquisition across all channels: paid social, paid search, SEO, email, content, and website

 - Plan, execute, and optimize paid campaigns with a focus on CAC, ROAS, and conversion

 - Continuously test and iterate - creatives, audiences, landing pages, messaging - to improve performance

 - Build and optimize the end-to-end customer journey from first touch to repeat purchase

 - Develop email/SMS lifecycle marketing to drive retention and LTV

 - Analyze performance data daily and make rapid decisions to scale what works

 - Collaborate with BioDAOs to craft compelling product stories and launch campaigns

 - Manage and grow organic channels including social media, SEO, and community

 - Support international fulfillment setup as we scale into new markets




REQUIREMENTS

 - 5+ years of health, wellness or supplement e-commerce experience at a D2C brand, with hands-on customer acquisition responsibility

 - Proven track record of scaling paid channels (Meta, Google, TikTok) profitably

 - Deep expertise in omnichannel marketing - paid, organic, email, website optimization

 - Obsessive about optimization: you test constantly and let data drive decisions

 - Strong analytical skills - fluent in CAC, LTV, ROAS, conversion funnels, and attribution

 - Experience building e-commerce operations from early stage to growth

 - Self-starter who thrives in fast-moving, autonomous remote environments







NICE TO HAVE

 - Familiarity with Shopify, Klaviyo, or headless commerce platforms

 - Interest in Web3, blockchain, or decentralized communities

 - Experience with influencer marketing or community-led growth




WHAT WE OFFER

 - Fully remote work with flexible hours

 - Competitive salary and token-based compensation

 - High ownership - you'll shape Biofy's growth strategy from day one

 - Mission-driven work: every sale supports groundbreaking scientific research

 - Collaborative team at the forefront of DeSci and biotechnology

source_jobs.title: Science Community Lead
source_jobs.description:
Bio is a decentralized science protocol that helps launch and grow AI-driven biotech research. It enables scientists to raise funds, create value from their work, and distribute that value directly to their communities. Since 2023, Bio has directed over $50m to global researchers, offering an alternative to traditional pharma funding. Backed by investors like Binance Labs, Northpond Ventures, and Animoca Brands, Bio accelerates real-world therapeutics across longevity, brain health, fertility, psychedelic science, and more.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We're looking for a Science Community Lead to build and nurture our community of scientists, researchers, and biotech enthusiasts. You'll be the connective tissue between our organization and the broader life sciences world - creating spaces where people want to hang out, learn, and collaborate.



This is a role perfect for someone who's passionate about science but wants to build community rather than spend their days at the bench. You'll own our Discord communities and be responsible for making them vibrant, welcoming, and genuinely useful to members.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Build and manage our Discord communities - creating channels, setting up bots, designing onboarding flows, and keeping conversations active

 - Host community events like AMAs, journal clubs, voice chats, and Twitter/X Spaces

 - Create programming that keeps members engaged and coming back - whether that's highlighting member research, running workshops, or sparking discussions around hot topics

 - Connect people - identify emerging voices, facilitate introductions, and help members find collaborators

 - Moderate discussions and maintain a welcoming, inclusive environment

 - Track community health metrics and experiment with new formats to drive engagement




WHO YOU ARE

 - You have a Bachelor's or Master's in biology, biochemistry, biotech, or a related field

 - You're Discord-native - you've spent real time in communities, understand the culture, and ideally have moderated a server before

 - You're probably already active in online science communities (Twitter/X, Reddit, Discord, etc.) and genuinely enjoy connecting with people

 - You have strong writing skills - warm, accessible, and human (not corporate)

 - You're comfortable hopping on voice chats and hosting live events

 - You take initiative and aren't afraid to try things that might not work

 - You're organized enough to keep multiple channels, events, and initiatives running smoothly




BONUS POINTS

 - Experience with Discord bots (MEE6, Carl-bot, or custom)

 - You run a science meme account, podcast, newsletter, or have built an online following

 - Familiarity with the biotech/life sciences landscape - who the players are, what people care about

 - Experience organizing in-person or virtual events




WHY JOIN US

 - Fully remote - work from anywhere

 - Be at the center of a growing community of scientists and builders

 - Shape the role and make it your own

 - Competitive compensation

source_jobs.title: Full-stack Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Bio is a decentralized science protocol that helps launch and grow AI-driven biotech research. It enables scientists to raise funds, create value from their work, and distribute that value directly to their communities. Since 2023, Bio has directed over $50m to global researchers, offering an alternative to traditional pharma funding. Backed by investors like Binance Labs, Northpond Ventures, and Animoca Brands, Bio accelerates real-world therapeutics across longevity, brain health, fertility, psychedelic science, and more.




THE ROLE

We're looking for a full-stack engineer to help build products that connect researchers, funders, and the broader scientific community. You'll work across the entire stack - from crafting intuitive user interfaces to designing robust backend systems and smart contracts.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

• Build and maintain web applications that serve researchers and the science community

• Design and implement APIs, databases, and backend services

• Collaborate with designers and product to ship features users love

• Contribute to web3 infrastructure and smart contract development

• Help shape technical direction and engineering culture as we grow




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

• Experience building web applications (any stack - we value adaptability over specific tools)

• Comfort working across frontend and backend

• Strong problem-solving skills and a pragmatic approach to shipping

• Clear communication - we're remote-first and async-friendly

• Curiosity about web3, biotech, or open science (experience not required)




NICE TO HAVE

• Experience with blockchain development or smart contracts

• Background in science, research, or working with scientific data

• Contributions to open source projects




WHY BIO

• Fully remote - work from anywhere

• Meaningful work at the frontier of science and technology

• Small team, big impact - your work matters here

• Competitive compensation with token/equity component

source_jobs.title: QA Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Bio is a decentralized science protocol that helps launch and grow AI-driven biotech research. It enables scientists to raise funds, create value from their work, and distribute that value directly to their communities. Since 2023, Bio has directed over $50m to global researchers, offering an alternative to traditional pharma funding. Backed by investors like Binance Labs, Northpond Ventures, and Animoca Brands, Bio accelerates real-world therapeutics across longevity, brain health, fertility, psychedelic science, and more.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We're building infrastructure for decentralized science and are looking for a QA Engineer who can own quality end-to-end - from manual testing and exploratory work to building automated test frameworks. This is a foundational role where you'll ensure our AI agents, web applications, and on-chain systems work reliably as we scale.

You'll work closely with engineering, product, and science teams to catch issues before they reach users and build the testing infrastructure that keeps us shipping fast with confidence.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Design and execute manual test plans for new features and releases

 - Build and maintain automated test suites (unit, integration, end-to-end)

 - Perform exploratory testing to find edge cases and unexpected issues

 - Integrate testing into CI/CD pipelines to catch regressions early

 - Test AI agent outputs for accuracy, consistency, and reliability

 - Collaborate with engineers to define test coverage and quality standards

 - Document bugs clearly and work with the team to prioritize fixes

 - Help establish QA best practices as the team grows




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - Proven experience in both manual testing and test automation

 - Strong background building automated test frameworks (Playwright, Cypress, Selenium, or similar)

 - Experience testing web applications and APIs

 - Experience testing AI/ML systems or non-deterministic outputs

 - Background in testing smart contracts or blockchain applications

 - Ability to think like a user - and like an attacker - to find issues others miss

 - Familiarity with CI/CD pipelines and integrating tests into deployment workflows

 - Comfortable operating in an early-stage, fast-moving environment

 - Strong communication skills and attention to detail




NICE TO HAVE

 - Prior work in biotech, research tools, or scientific software

 - Contributions to open-source testing tools or frameworks




WHY JOIN US

 - Foundational role with real ownership over quality

 - Competitive salary + meaningful equity/token component

 - Remote-first culture with a small, high-trust team

 - Opportunity to shape QA practices from day one

source_jobs.title: Science Content Writer
source_jobs.description:
Bio is a decentralized science protocol that helps launch and grow AI-driven biotech research. It enables scientists to raise funds, create value from their work, and distribute that value directly to their communities. Since 2023, Bio has directed over $50m to global researchers, offering an alternative to traditional pharma funding. Backed by investors like Binance Labs, Northpond Ventures, and Animoca Brands, Bio accelerates real-world therapeutics across longevity, brain health, fertility, psychedelic science, and more.





ABOUT THE ROLE

This is a role for someone who lives at the intersection of science and storytelling. You understand peptide research (or adjacent fields), you’re fluent in how scientific communities engage on X and LinkedIn, and you can turn a protein folding breakthrough into a post that gets 300k views. You’ll work closely with our growth team and scientific leads to build this project’s presence from the ground up.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Own the social media strategy and content calendar for scientific projects, such as the peptide agent project as well as Bios, primarily on X (Twitter) and LinkedIn

 - Create thought leadership content that positions the project as a serious scientific endeavor - technical blog posts, discovery announcements, case studies, and explainer threads

 - Translate complex peptide science and AI research workflows into accessible, engaging social content that resonates with researchers and the scientific community

 - Develop and produce visual content including protein folding animations, data visualizations, and scientific graphics to accompany posts

 - Coordinate with scientific leads (e.g. our Head of AI) to surface real discoveries and breakthroughs worth amplifying

 - Support co-marketing efforts with partner organizations (e.g. Lightfold, Adaptive Bio) and coordinate cross-posting with the Bio main account and BIOS

 - Monitor engagement metrics, experiment with formats, and iterate on what drives attention in the scientific and AI research community

 - Use AI tools to scale content production - not to replace quality, but to multiply your output


WHO YOU ARE

 - You have a background in biology, biochemistry, biotech, pharmacology, or a related scientific field (Bachelor’s or Master’s)

 - You’re already active in scientific communities on X, Reddit, LinkedIn, or similar platforms and understand what content pulls attention

 - You have demonstrable experience creating scientific or technical content for social media - whether for a company, a personal brand, a newsletter, or a research group

 - You understand the peptide/drug discovery space, or are close enough to get up to speed quickly (e.g. adjacent experience in pharma, biotech, computational biology)

 - You write clearly and compellingly - warm, smart, and human, not corporate

 - You’re comfortable working with AI tools and can use them strategically to scale your work

 - You take initiative, move fast, and don’t need hand-holding - you’ll be building this from scratch


BONUS POINTS

 - You understand AI and AI GTM for start-ups

 - You run (or have run) a science-focused social account, podcast, newsletter, or meme page with a real following

 - Experience with video content - short-form clips, protein folding visualizations, screen recordings of research tools

 - Familiarity with the biotech/life sciences landscape - who the key players are, what people care about, what’s trending

 - Understanding of DeSci (decentralized science) or Web3, though the role itself is explicitly non-crypto-facing

 - Experience producing or contributing to technical blog posts, whitepapers, or archive-style case studies




DETAILS

 - Fully remote - work from anywhere

notes: Highly technical roles, AI-driven biotech research, decentralized science protocol
